title: Is Caleb Durbin’s turnaround enough to solidify his future with the Red Sox?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Caleb Durbin led the Red Sox in OPS (.977) and home runs (7) over the past 30 days.
Durbin's performance improvement followed work with a new personal hitting coach mid-season.
Boston retains team control of Durbin until 2031, providing salary cap flexibility for future moves.

title: Red Sox’ Aroldis Chapman breaks MLB relief pitcher strikeout record in save vs. Angels
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox's Aroldis Chapman sets MLB record with 1,364 strikeouts as a relief pitcher, surpassing Hoyt Wilhelm.
Chapman achieved the record in 847 innings, significantly fewer than Wilhelm's 1,872.1 innings.
Chapman, at 38, is a two-time World Series champion and could be traded before the August 3 deadline.

title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston ranks as the most collision-prone city in the U.S., with drivers averaging 3.7 years between accidents.
Worcester and Springfield also rank low, with 5.14 and 5.18 years between crashes, respectively.
Providence, RI, is among the 10 riskiest cities, with an average of 5.87 years between collisions.

title: Falmouth man, 74, arrested after police say he led troopers on wrong-way, high-speed chase over Bourne Bridge
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Armando Deleon, 74, was arrested after leading a wrong-way, high-speed chase over Bourne Bridge at 105 mph.
The chase began when Deleon's vehicle nearly hit emergency personnel on Route 25 eastbound in Bourne.
Deleon faces over a dozen charges including assault with a dangerous weapon and driving with a suspended license.

title: Boston police release new photos of alleged lemonade stand robber
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police released photos of an unidentified juvenile suspect in a South Boston lemonade stand robbery.
The robbery involved two suspects; a 14-year-old was previously arrested and charged with armed robbery.
Police request public assistance to identify the second suspect involved in the June 10 incident.

title: Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens appear on Rich Paul’s big board of LeBron James landing spots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens were mentioned by Rich Paul as a potential landing spot for LeBron James.
LeBron James is leaving the Lakers and reportedly considering contending franchises, including Celtics for his next team.
Celtics added Robinson, Conley Jr., and George to strengthen their roster in hopes of returning to the NBA Finals.

title: Robert Parish not a fan of Celtics’ decision to trade Jaylen Brown: ‘If Red [Auerbach] was alive … I think this would have made him nauseated’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Robert Parish criticizes the Celtics' decision to trade Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers, comparing it to the Mavericks' trade of Luka Doncic.
Parish expressed his belief that former Celtics executive Red Auerbach would have strongly disapproved of the Brown trade.
Parish hopes for no animosity between Brown and the Celtics and envisions Brown's jersey being retired eventually.

title: Jayson Tatum bids farewell to Jaylen Brown: ‘Nothing but love and respect for you’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum bids farewell to Jaylen Brown, expressing gratitude for their nine seasons together with the Boston Celtics.
Jaylen Brown was traded to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George and four draft picks, per Shams Charania.
Brown expressed feeling disrespected by the Celtics and was reportedly surprised by the trade decision.

title: Celtics, Neemias Queta reportedly agree to four-year extension
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics extend Neemias Queta's contract for four years at $56 million, finalizing through the 2030-31 season.
Queta was one of last season's most improved NBA players, finishing fourth in Most Improved Player voting.
Queta's new deal follows Jaylen Brown's trade to the 76ers and Mitchell Robinson's signing with Boston.

title: Union Oyster House turns 200: Inside America’s oldest continuously operating restaurant
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston celebrates 200 years as America's oldest continuously operating restaurant.
The restaurant's building, recognized as a National Historic Landmark, dates back to 1716 or 1717.
Master Chef Americo DiFronzo serves about 1,000 to 1,500 guests daily, with clam chowder being a popular item.
